support.definition: Help given to someone, either practical or emotional.
support.example: New parents need a lot of support from family.

responsiveness.definition: Being quick to notice and react to someone's needs.
responsiveness.example: A parent's warm responsiveness helps the baby feel safe.

exhausted.definition: Extremely tired; having no energy left.
exhausted.example: After the long flight, she was completely exhausted.

depleted.definition: Used up; having nothing left to give.
depleted.example: She felt emotionally depleted after weeks of no sleep.

reserves.definition: Extra strength or energy that you can use when needed.
reserves.example: You will be amazed by the reserves of energy you find.

indefinitely.definition: For a time with no known end; forever.
indefinitely.example: You cannot keep going indefinitely without rest.

sustainable.definition: Able to continue for a long time without causing problems.
sustainable.example: Working sixteen hours a day is not sustainable.

practical.definition: Related to real, everyday tasks rather than feelings or ideas.
practical.example: She needed practical help like cooking and cleaning.

therapist.definition: A trained person who helps you talk about and manage your feelings.
therapist.example: She visited a therapist to talk about her anxiety.

ambiguity.definition: When something can be understood in more than one way; mixed feelings.
ambiguity.example: Parenting often comes with ambiguity about what is right.

adversely.definition: In a harmful or negative way.
adversely.example: The stress did not adversely affect his health.

intrusive.definition: Entering your mind when you do not want it to; unwelcome.
intrusive.example: She had intrusive thoughts that scared her.

plucked.definition: Gathered or found (courage); pulled together bravery.
plucked.example: She plucked up her courage and spoke to the doctor.

intention.definition: A plan or purpose to do something.
intention.example: She had no intention of hurting anyone.

influenced.definition: Changed or affected by something or someone.
influenced.example: He was influenced by his mother's positive attitude.

grip.definition: A strong hold or control over something.
grip.example: The scary thoughts started to lose their grip on her.

cathartic.definition: Providing a release of strong emotions that makes you feel better.
cathartic.example: Crying can be cathartic when you are very stressed.

dispel.definition: To make something go away; to remove fears or worries.
dispel.example: Talking about your fears can help dispel them.

container.definition: Here, a person who can hold your feelings safely without being overwhelmed.
container.chinese: 容器（比喻能承接情緒的人）
container.example: A good friend acts as a container for your worries.

overwhelmed.definition: Feeling that there is too much to deal with; completely overcome.
overwhelmed.example: She felt overwhelmed by all the responsibilities.

optimism.definition: The belief that things will turn out well.
optimism.example: Her gentle optimism helped me get through the hard times.

spectrum.definition: A range between two extreme points.
spectrum.example: Feelings can fall anywhere on the spectrum from happy to sad.

doom.definition: A feeling that something very bad is going to happen.
doom.example: She was filled with a sense of doom about the future.

spell.definition: To explain something clearly in detail.
spell.example: You may have to spell out what kind of help you need.

tribe.definition: A group of people who support and care for each other.
tribe.example: Humans are pack animals who need a tribe.

mute.example: He stood there, mute and unable to express his feelings.

finance.definition: To provide money for something; to pay for.
finance.example: It is hard to finance a family on one salary.

generation.definition: All the people born around the same time, like parents or grandparents.
generation.example: The younger generation faces different challenges.

rectify.definition: To fix or correct something that is wrong.
rectify.example: The government should rectify this unfairness.

renting.definition: Paying money to use a house or apartment that belongs to someone else.
renting.example: Renting an apartment in the city is very expensive.

emotionally.definition: In a way that relates to feelings.
emotionally.example: She supported her daughter emotionally during the hard time.

stylist.definition: A person whose job is to create a fashionable look for people or things.
stylist.example: She works part-time as a fashion stylist.

twins.definition: Two babies born at the same time from the same mother.
twins.example: She was surprised to find out she was having twins.

thriving.definition: Growing and developing well; healthy and strong.
thriving.example: One of the twins was not thriving as expected.

traumatic.definition: Very shocking and upsetting, causing emotional pain.
traumatic.example: The birth was traumatic for both the mother and babies.

incubated.definition: Kept in a special warm machine in the hospital to help a weak baby grow.
incubated.example: The premature baby had to be incubated for two weeks.

poorly.example: The poorly baby needed special care in the hospital.

tour.definition: A journey to different places, especially for work or performing.
tour.example: The musician was often away on tour.

asserted.example: The often-asserted belief that men must be strong is harmful.

cognitive.definition: Related to thinking and understanding with the mind.
cognitive.example: She knew on a cognitive level that the baby was hers.

nanny.definition: A person paid to care for children in the family's home.
nanny.example: They hired a nanny to help with the baby.

nursery.definition: A room or place for taking care of young children.
nursery.example: The nursery nurse stayed with the baby all day.

distracted.definition: Turned attention away from something; not focused.
distracted.example: She distracted herself by going out instead of facing her feelings.

clubbing.definition: Going to dance clubs for entertainment at night.
clubbing.example: She went clubbing every weekend to forget her problems.

denial.definition: Refusing to accept that something is true or happened.
denial.example: She was in denial about being the mother of twins.

comforting.definition: Making someone feel better when they are sad or upset.
comforting.example: She never felt like comforting the crying baby.

soothing.definition: Calming and making someone feel less upset.
soothing.example: She should have been soothing him through his distress.

clingy.definition: Always wanting to be near someone; too dependent.
clingy.example: The anxious child became very clingy with his mother.

compounds.definition: Makes something worse by adding to it.
compounds.example: His feeling of rejection compounds his insecurity.

insecurity.definition: A feeling of not being safe or confident in relationships.
insecurity.example: His insecurity about relationships affected his friendships.

bonding.definition: The process of forming a close emotional connection.
bonding.example: Skin-to-skin contact helps with bonding after birth.

feat.definition: Something that is hard to do; an achievement.
feat.example: Finding one-on-one time is no easy feat with four children.

relishing.example: She is relishing her special time with her son.

mending.definition: Fixing or repairing something that is broken.
mending.example: She is mending the early rupture in their relationship.

diminishes.example: As his sense of security grows, his longing diminishes.

sponges.definition: Things that absorb or take in a lot; babies absorb everything around them.
sponges.chinese: 海綿（比喻容易吸收的人）
sponges.example: Babies are like sponges, absorbing everything they experience.

re-form.definition: To change and develop again in a new way.
re-form.example: We can re-form ourselves through new relationships.

romantic.definition: Related to love between two partners.
romantic.example: His romantic relationships were affected by his childhood.

attachments.definition: Emotional bonds or connections to other people.
attachments.example: His early attachments shaped how he relates to others.

longing.definition: A deep feeling of wanting something or someone very much.
longing.example: He felt a deep longing to be close to his mother.

anguished.definition: Feeling extreme pain or suffering in the heart.
anguished.example: The anguished father wanted to help but did not know how.

impressionable.definition: Easily influenced or affected by experiences.
impressionable.example: Babies are incredibly impressionable in their early months.

fulfil.definition: To satisfy or meet a need completely.
fulfil.example: The nanny was expected to fulfil all the baby's needs.

entrenched.definition: Deeply established and very hard to change.
entrenched.example: These cultural patterns are so entrenched that they seem natural.

indoctrinated.definition: Taught to accept a set of beliefs without questioning them.
indoctrinated.example: People can be indoctrinated into thinking a certain way is natural.

indoctrinations.definition: Beliefs that were taught to you without you questioning them.
indoctrinations.example: Cultural indoctrinations can get in the way of good parenting.

functional.definition: Working well; able to operate properly.
functional.example: Therapy can lead to a more functional way of living.

upbringing.definition: The way you were raised and cared for as a child.
upbringing.example: Her upbringing shaped who she is today.

foster.definition: A person who takes care of a child that is not their own, temporarily.
foster.example: The foster parent gave the child a loving home.

adoptive.definition: Related to legally taking another person's child as your own.
adoptive.example: His adoptive parents loved him as their own.

guardian.definition: A person who is legally responsible for taking care of a child.
guardian.example: After her parents passed away, her uncle became her guardian.

surrogate.definition: Acting in place of someone else; a substitute parent.
surrogate.example: Her grandmother acted as a surrogate parent.

anchor.definition: Something that gives a feeling of safety and stability.
anchor.example: A strong bond acts as a safe anchor in a child's life.

priority.definition: Something that is more important than other things.
priority.example: Children need to feel they are a priority for their parents.

delegated.definition: Given to someone else to do; handed off.
delegated.example: Parenting should not be delegated entirely to paid helpers.

symbol.definition: A picture or shape that stands for something else.
symbol.example: Draw a symbol to represent yourself on the paper.

diagram.definition: A simple drawing that shows how things are connected.
diagram.example: Draw a diagram of your support network.

solid.definition: Strong and firm; not broken or dotted.
solid.example: Draw solid lines for support that comes naturally.

dotted.definition: Made of a series of small dots instead of a continuous line.
dotted.example: Use dotted lines for support you have to arrange yourself.

gaps.definition: Empty spaces; things that are missing.
gaps.example: Look for any gaps in your support network.

dependent.definition: Needing someone else to take care of you.
dependent.example: Young children are dependent on their parents.

counselling.definition: Professional help where someone talks to you about your problems.
counselling.example: She wished she had gotten counselling earlier.

impaired.definition: Weakened or damaged; not working as well as it should.
impaired.example: Her ability to feel for others was impaired by her own pain.

rupture.definition: A break or damage in a connection or relationship.
rupture.example: The early rupture between mother and child can be repaired.
